channel.title: Context Engineering Daily - Research Papers
channel.description: Latest Research Papers news in Context Engineering

item.title: Summarize Before You Speak with ARACH: A Training-Free Inference-Time Plug-In for Enhancing LLMs via Global Attention Reallocation
item.description: arXiv:2603.11067v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Large language models (LLMs) achieve remarkable performance, yet further gains often require costly training. This has motivated growing interest in post-training techniques-especially training-free approaches that improve models at inference time wit...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> attention, LLM, prompt, large language model, context | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: DeReason: A Difficulty-Aware Curriculum Improves Decoupled SFT-then-RL Training for General Reasoning
item.description: arXiv:2603.11193v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Reinforcement learning with Verifiable Rewards (RLVR) has emerged as a powerful paradigm for eliciting reasoning capabilities in large language models, particularly in mathematics and coding. While recent efforts have extended this paradigm to broader...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> reasoning, paper, fine-tuning, LLM, large language model | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Markovian Generation Chains in Large Language Models
item.description: arXiv:2603.11228v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: The widespread use of large language models (LLMs) raises an important question: how do texts evolve when they are repeatedly processed by LLMs? In this paper, we define this iterative inference process as Markovian generation chains, where each step ...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> paper, LLM, model, template, prompt | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Artificial Intelligence for Sentiment Analysis of Persian Poetry
item.description: arXiv:2603.11254v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Recent advancements of the Artificial Intelligence (AI) have led to the development of large language models (LLMs) that are capable of understanding, analysing, and creating textual data. These language models open a significant opportunity in analyz...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> transformer, LLM, GPT, analysis, large language model | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Evaluating Explainable AI Attribution Methods in Neural Machine Translation via Attention-Guided Knowledge Distillation
item.description: arXiv:2603.11342v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: The study of the attribution of input features to the output of neural network models is an active area of research. While numerous Explainable AI (XAI) techniques have been proposed to interpret these models, the systematic and automated evaluation o...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> paper, transformer, attention, alignment, library | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Stop Listening to Me! How Multi-turn Conversations Can Degrade Diagnostic Reasoning
item.description: arXiv:2603.11394v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Patients and clinicians are increasingly using chatbots powered by large language models (LLMs) for healthcare inquiries. While state-of-the-art LLMs exhibit high performance on static diagnostic reasoning benchmarks, their efficacy across multi-turn ...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> reasoning, paper, LLM, framework, large language model | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: DIVE: Scaling Diversity in Agentic Task Synthesis for Generalizable Tool Use
item.description: arXiv:2603.11076v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Recent work synthesizes agentic tasks for post-training tool-using LLMs, yet robust generalization under shifts in tasks and toolsets remains an open challenge. We trace this brittleness to insufficient diversity in synthesized tasks. Scaling diversit...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> LLM, analysis, RAG, tool, arxiv | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: The Unlearning Mirage: A Dynamic Framework for Evaluating LLM Unlearning
item.description: arXiv:2603.11266v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Unlearning in Large Language Models (LLMs) aims to enhance safety, mitigate biases, and comply with legal mandates, such as the right to be forgotten. However, existing unlearning methods are brittle: minor query modifications, such as multi-hop reaso...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> reasoning, LLM, framework, large language model, release | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: AI Psychometrics: Evaluating the Psychological Reasoning of Large Language Models with Psychometric Validities
item.description: arXiv:2603.11279v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: The immense number of parameters and deep neural networks make large language models (LLMs) rival the complexity of human brains, which also makes them opaque ``black box'' systems that are challenging to evaluate and interpret. AI Psychometrics is an...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> reasoning, paper, LLM, GPT, large language model | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Counterweights and Complementarities: The Convergence of AI and Blockchain Powering a Decentralized Future
item.description: arXiv:2603.11299v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: This editorial addresses the critical intersection of artificial intelligence (AI) and blockchain technologies, highlighting their contrasting tendencies toward centralization and decentralization, respectively. While AI, particularly with the rise of...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> LLM, large language model, research, model, arxiv | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Structure-Aware Epistemic Uncertainty Quantification for Neural Operator PDE Surrogates
item.description: arXiv:2603.11052v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Neural operators (NOs) provide fast, resolution-invariant surrogates for mapping input fields to PDE solution fields, but their predictions can exhibit significant epistemic uncertainty due to finite data, imperfect optimization, and distribution shif...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> experiment, alignment, RAG, arxiv | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Task-Conditioned Routing Signatures in Sparse Mixture-of-Experts Transformers
item.description: arXiv:2603.11114v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Sparse Mixture-of-Experts (MoE) architectures enable efficient scaling of large language models through conditional computation, yet the routing mechanisms responsible for expert selection remain poorly understood. In this work, we introduce routing s...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> transformer, prompt, large language model, analysis, release | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 100%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Speculative Decoding Scaling Laws (SDSL): Throughput Optimization Made Simple
item.description: arXiv:2603.11053v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Speculative decoding is a technique that uses multiple language models to accelerate infer- ence. Previous works have used an experi- mental approach to optimize the throughput of the inference pipeline, which involves LLM training and can be costly. ...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> model, study, LLM, arxiv | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 80%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: A Survey of Reasoning in Autonomous Driving Systems: Open Challenges and Emerging Paradigms
item.description: arXiv:2603.11093v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: The development of high-level autonomous driving (AD) is shifting from perception-centric limitations to a more fundamental bottleneck, namely, a deficit in robust and generalizable reasoning. Although current AD systems manage structured environments...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> reasoning, LLM, multimodal, framework, analysis | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 80%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Reversible Lifelong Model Editing via Semantic Routing-Based LoRA
item.description: arXiv:2603.11239v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: The dynamic evolution of real-world necessitates model editing within Large Language Models. While existing methods explore modular isolation or parameter-efficient strategies, they still suffer from semantic drift or knowledge forgetting due to conti...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> framework, large language model, model, experiment, arxiv | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 80%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Mind the Sim2Real Gap in User Simulation for Agentic Tasks
item.description: arXiv:2603.11245v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: As NLP evaluation shifts from static benchmarks to multi-turn interactive settings, LLM-based simulators have become widely used as user proxies, serving two roles: generating user turns and providing evaluation signals. Yet, these simulations are fre...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> model, study, LLM, arxiv | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 80%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Comparison of Outlier Detection Algorithms on String Data
item.description: arXiv:2603.11049v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Outlier detection is a well-researched and crucial problem in machine learning. However, there is little research on string data outlier detection, as most literature focuses on outlier detection of numerical data. A robust string data outlier detecti...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> research, experiment, arxiv | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 60%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: A Learning-Based Superposition Operator for Non-Renewal Arrival Processes in Queueing Networks
item.description: arXiv:2603.11118v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: The superposition of arrival processes is a fundamental yet analytically intractable operation in queueing networks when inputs are general non-renewal streams. Classical methods either reduce merged flows to renewal surrogates, rely on computationall...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> framework, analysis, model, experiment, arxiv | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 60%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: High-resolution weather-guided surrogate modeling for data-efficient cross-location building energy prediction
item.description: arXiv:2603.11121v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Building design optimization often depends on physics-based simulation tools such as EnergyPlus, which, although accurate, are computationally expensive and slow. Surrogate models provide a faster alternative, yet most are location-specific, and even ...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> experiment, model, study, tool, arxiv | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 60%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)

item.title: Group Resonance Network: Learnable Prototypes and Multi-Subject Resonance for EEG Emotion Recognition
item.description: arXiv:2603.11119v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Electroencephalography(EEG)-basedemotionrecognitionre- mains challenging in cross-subject settings due to severe inter-subject variability. Existing methods mainly learn subject-invariant features, but often under-exploit stimulus-locked group regular...<br><br><small><strong>Source:</strong> arXiv | <strong>Keywords:</strong> model, experiment, arxiv | <strong>Relevance:</strong> 40%</small>
item.author: noreply@contextengineering.news (arXiv)
